principal.description: The security principal identifier `{type}:{id}`. A principal is the entity that receives a role. The supported types are `user`, `user-group`, and `api-client`.

id.description: The unique identifier of the principal.

role.description: The unique role identifier in GRN syntax. A role is a collection of permissions defining what actions the principal can perform.

id.description: The unique identifier of the role.

scope.description: Fully qualified scope string in GRN syntax. A scope is the specific resource or set of resources to which the role and its permissions apply. The scopes can be at the workspace level (limited to 1), tenant groups (up to 10), and scope groups (up to 10), and can be combined: `Workspace + Tenant Group` or `Tenant Group + Scope Group`.

id.description: The unique identifier of the object. Immutable.

name.description: Friendly name to be displayed to users. Allowed characters are [a-z A-Z 0-9 -(dash) (space)].

releaseStage.description: It is only defined for `PREDEFINED` roles.

slug.description: Empty for `CUSTOM` roles. Unique name for `PREDEFINED` roles and the pattern is provider name followed by the actual name (`<providerName>.<name>`).

grn.description: The HPE GreenLake Resource Notation (GRN) is the standardized, URI-compatible syntax used to define and specify this scope within the HPE GreenLake.

name.description: Must be unique within the context it is being created (organization or organization + workspace). Most ASCII characters are allowed, except [`&`, `<`, `>`, `|`, `:`, `,`] and leading or trailing space.

info.description: The HPE GreenLake for Authorization API provides a unified way to manage the authorization function for HPE GreenLake cloud.
info.title: HPE GreenLake for Authorization API

get.description:
Retrieves role assignments by applying [OData 4.0](https://www.odata.org/documentation/) filters. Use the `filter` parameter to provide a filter string. Supports `in` and `and` operators on `role`, `scope` and `principal` attributes.
*Example Request:*
`/authorization/v1beta1/role-assignments?filter=role in ('grn:glp/providers/authorization/roles/storageservices.LimitedAdmin') and principal in ('user:123981y2zxhiz1890')`

**Note:**
- **No duplicate attributes in OData filter**: Each attribute (`role`, `scope`, `principal`) can only appear once in the OData filter expression. Multiple occurrences will result in a `400 Bad Request` error.
- **Supported operators**: Only the `in` and `and` operators are supported.

parameters.description:
The filter query parameter is used to filter the set of resources returned in a collection-level GET. The returned set of resources matches the criteria in the filter query parameter.

Supports `in` and `and` operators on `role`, `scope` and `principal` attributes.
parameters.name: filter

post.description:
Create a role assignment.

By creating a role assignment, you grant access to resources. Role assignments allow for controlled management of permissions and responsibilities.

**Note:** For assistance finding the `principal`, `scope`, and `role` identifiers, see the [developer guide](https://developer.greenlake.hpe.com/docs/greenlake/services/authorization/guide#making-it-all-work).

Location.description: URL to the newly created resource.

delete.summary: Delete a role assignment instance by ID

get.summary: Retrieve a role assignment instance by ID

parameters.description: The role assignment instance identifier. The ID can be found in the response body of POST `/authorization/v1beta1/role-assignments`.
parameters.name: id

put.description:
Allows updating scopes of an existing role assignment. Request body must contain `id`, `principal` and `role` attributes even though they are immutable.

The `id` can be found in the response body of POST `/authorization/v1beta1/role-assignments`.

**Note:** For assistance finding the `principal`, `scope`, and `role` identifiers, see the [developer guide](https://developer.greenlake.hpe.com/docs/greenlake/services/authorization/guide#making-it-all-work).
put.summary: Update a role assignment instance by ID

get.description:
Retrieves scope groups by applying [OData 4.0](https://www.odata.org/documentation/) filters. Use the `filter` parameter to provide a filter string. Supports `in` operator on `serviceMetadata/id`, `name` or `grn` attributes.
*Example Request:*
`/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups?filter=grn in ('grn:glp/workspaces/05f0523c-fd03-47fc-981b-9c4333a37b70/regions/default/providers/authorization/scope-groups/123')`

**Note:**
- **No duplicate attributes in OData filter**: Each attribute (`serviceMetadata/id`, `name`, `grn`) can only appear once in the OData filter expression. Multiple occurrences will result in a `400 Bad Request` error.
- **Only one attribute in OData filter**: Only one attribute (`serviceMetadata/id`, `name` or `grn`) can be used at a time, otherwise it will result in a `400 Bad Request` error.
- **Supported operators**: Only `in` operator is supported.

parameters.description: Total number of results to be returned. If the parameter is not provided, it will return all records found.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: OData style filter for filtering scope groups. Supports `in` operator on `serviceMetadata/id`, `name` or `grn` attributes.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort results by a single attribute and allow setting a sorting direction as ascending (asc) or descending (desc). Sorting is valid only for `name` attribute. Default sorting direction if omitted is ascending.
parameters.name: sort

post.description:
A scope group is a collection of scopes that can be assigned to a role assignment. This allows a single role assignment to cover multiple scopes.

**Note:**
  • A scope group cannot contain another scope group (no nesting). Once created, a scope group will belong to a organization/workspace based on the caller context. The returned `grn` attribute will include this context and can be used as a global unique identifier.

Location.description: URL to the newly created resource.

delete.summary: Delete a scope group instance by ID

get.summary: Retrieve a scope group instance by ID

parameters.description: The scope group instance identifier. The ID can be found in the response body of POST `/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups`.
parameters.name: id

put.description: Request body must contain `id` attribute even though it is immutable.
put.summary: Update a scope group instance by ID

get.description: Retrieves a list of scopes for a specific scope group. Results are sorted by description and GRN alphabetically by default.

parameters.description: Total number of results to be returned. If the parameter is not provided, it will return all records found.
parameters.name: limit

get.summary: Retrieve the scope group scopes list

parameters.description: The scope group instance identifier. The ID can be found in the response body of POST `/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups`.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The scope group instance identifier. ID can be found in the response body of POST `/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups`.
parameters.name: id

post.description: Add new scopes to an existing scope group. This operation is synchronous and non-atomic.
post.summary: Add scopes to a scope group

delete.description: Delete scopes from an existing scope group. The scope group ID can be found in the response body of GET `/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups` while the scope ID to be deleted can be found in the response body of GET `/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups/{id}/scopes`. This operation is synchronous and non-atomic.
delete.summary: Delete scopes from a scope group

parameters.description: The scope group instance identifier. ID can be found in the response body of POST `/authorization/v1beta1/scope-groups`.
parameters.name: id

tags.description:
Role assignments are composed of three pieces (principal, role, and scope). Role assigments associate a user, group, or service (principal) with a specific role (along with its permissions) at a particular scope (a resource or group of resources) to grant them access and specify their responsibilities within HPE GreenLake.

**Note:** There is a maximum limit of 50 role assignments per user per workspace.

**Note:** Users can manage roles and retrieve a role identifier (ID / GRN) using the Web Interface. Once the role ID / GRN is retrieved then a role assignment can be created using the API described below.
tags.name: Role Assignments

tags.description:
A scope group is composed of scopes and allows a single role assignment against multiple scopes.

Known limitations
  • There is a max limit of 500 scopes per scope group.
  • There is a max limit of 500 scope groups per workspace.
tags.name: Scope Groups
